twitter_thread: 30% of software budgets now go to AI integration, forcing 40% of companies to halt other projects.
Retraining staff on TensorFlow and PyTorch costs $10k per employee, a hidden expense many overlook.
Average AI budget allocation jumped from 10% in 2020 to 25% today, a 150% increase in just 3 years.
4 in 10 companies put projects on hold due to AI budget constraints, a stark consequence of AI adoption.
Assuming AI replaces existing software is a $100k mistake, as it often requires new infrastructure investments.
IBM's warning: AI integration costs can cripple other software projects, a risk many companies can't afford.
Have you had to sacrifice other projects for AI integration? What was the cost? #aiadoption #softwarebudgets
pack.linkedin: IBM's warning that 30% of software budgets are now spent on AI integration has significant implications. With average AI budget allocation increasing 150% in 3 years, many companies are forced to put other projects on hold. Retraining staff on tools like TensorFlow and PyTorch adds up to $10k per employee. Assuming AI replaces existing software can be a $100k mistake. How can companies balance AI adoption with other software priorities, and what's the real cost of getting it wrong?
tiktok_hooks: 30% of software budgets are being eaten by AI, what's the snack on your project?
Buying AI? You're actually buying a whole new IT infrastructure, too
AI is putting 40% of software projects on ice, will yours be next?
pack.reddit_headline: 40% of companies halt projects due to AI budget constraints: is this the new normal?
